metadata.scope: Synthetic source pack for a short internal decision memo about whether to continue a review-cycle operating pilot.

inferred_research_questions.question: Did review cycle time improve within the synthetic pilot scope?
inferred_research_questions.why_it_matters: The primary recommendation depends on a bounded time-improvement claim.
search_queries: synthetic review cycle time pilot baseline endpoint sample

inferred_research_questions.question: Did rework decrease without overextending the conclusion?
inferred_research_questions.why_it_matters: The memo must distinguish operating signal from enterprise proof.
search_queries: synthetic rework item pilot sample limitation

research_plan.depth_rationale: Standard research is sufficient because this is a short internal memo, but numerical claims require explicit metric support.
research_plan.source_mode_rationale: This committed fixture uses synthetic records so the example remains anonymized and stable.
research_plan.user_feedback: Create a quantitative example that exercises baseline, denominator, timeframe, fact-check, and bounded recommendation handling.

research_brief.executive_summary: The synthetic source pack supports a narrow continuation recommendation. Review cycle time moved from 10 days to 7 days across 20 sampled review packets over one quarter, and average rework items moved from 6 to 4 per packet over the same sample. The evidence does not support enterprise ROI, compliance, or broad productivity claims.
key_findings: The time-improvement claim is supportable only with baseline, endpoint, sample, and timeframe attached.
The rework claim is directionally useful but still pilot-scoped.
The sample is too narrow for enterprise ROI or benchmark language.
The next quarter should test repeatability on a broader review mix.

source_registry.title: Synthetic Pilot Metrics Log
source_registry.bias_or_agenda: Internal pilot log may favor operational improvement narrative.
source_registry.notes: Records 20 sampled review packets over one quarter, with baseline and endpoint cycle time and rework counts.

claim_support.rationale: Contains the sampled packet count, one-quarter window, and cycle-time baseline and endpoint.
Contains the same sample and the rework baseline and endpoint.
Shows improvement signals that could be overread if not bounded by the caution source.
Supports continuing the pilot, but not the exact named next-quarter categories by itself.

source_registry.title: Synthetic Review Queue Timing Extract
source_registry.bias_or_agenda: Operational extract captures queue timing but not downstream outcome quality.
source_registry.notes: Supports review cycle time moving from 10 days to 7 days over the pilot sample.

claim_support.rationale: Confirms the queue timing movement from 10 days to 7 days.
Could be misused to imply broader proof if cited without the caution source.

source_registry.title: Synthetic Rework Item Review
source_registry.bias_or_agenda: Practitioner-style metric review emphasizes process signal.
source_registry.notes: Supports average rework items moving from 6 to 4 per packet in the same sample.

claim_support.rationale: Supports the rework movement as a process metric.
Supports continuing the pilot as a process-learning step, but not broad rollout.

source_registry.title: Synthetic Pilot Extrapolation Caution Note
source_registry.bias_or_agenda: Cautionary source emphasizes sampling limits and repeatability risk.
source_registry.notes: Warns that a one-quarter pilot should not be converted into enterprise ROI, compliance, or benchmark claims.

claim_support.rationale: Supports keeping the cycle-time claim bounded to the pilot sample.
Supports keeping the rework claim bounded to the pilot sample.
Directly supports the caveat against ROI, compliance, or benchmark claims.
Supports a controlled continuation rather than broad rollout.

evidence_matrix.claim: In the synthetic one-quarter pilot, median review cycle time moved from 10 days to 7 days, a 30 percent reduction across 20 sampled review packets.
evidence_matrix.notes: Strong only when the sentence includes the baseline, endpoint, sample size, and one-quarter timeframe.

evidence_matrix.claim: Average rework items moved from 6 to 4 per packet, a 33 percent reduction across the same 20 sampled review packets over one quarter.
evidence_matrix.notes: Supportable as an internal pilot metric, not as a benchmark.

evidence_matrix.claim: The pilot evidence is not sufficient to claim enterprise ROI, compliance improvement, or broad productivity improvement.
The next quarter should continue the pilot for payment exception triage, access reviews, and model-change packets before any broader rollout claim.
evidence_matrix.notes: Recommendation is bounded and names concrete next review categories.

source_summaries.S1: Pilot metric log records 10-day to 7-day movement.
source_summaries.S2: Queue timing extract supports the same time movement.

rows.implication: Use the number with full context and avoid generalizing it.

source_summaries.S3: Review notes support rework reduction as process signal.

rows.implication: The memo can use the rework metric as supporting evidence.

source_summaries.S4: A one-quarter sample cannot support ROI or compliance claims.

rows.implication: Keep the recommendation to another quarter, not broad rollout.

contradictions.issue: Pilot improvement may not repeat with more complex review packets.
contradictions.handling: Keep pilot scope and sample size in the sentence.

contradictions.issue: Lower rework may reflect easier packet mix rather than process quality.
contradictions.handling: Call it a pilot signal, not proof.

open_questions: Does the next quarter include more complex review packet categories?
Can finance validate any cost or ROI claim after a larger sample?

draft_support_notes.cautions: Do not remove baseline, sample, or timeframe.

facts_safe_to_use.fact: Median review cycle time moved from 10 days to 7 days across 20 sampled review packets over one quarter.
Average rework items moved from 6 to 4 per packet across the same sample.

claims_to_soften.claim: The pilot proves the operating model is better.
claims_to_soften.handling: Change to pilot metrics support continuing the controlled test.
